political.s: Wyndham Hotels demonstrates mixed political engagement with modest direct PAC contributions. 2024 cycle contributions were $26,116 with lobbying spending of $270,000. Company maintains bipartisan approach focusing on industry issues rather than partisan causes.

charity.s: Wyndham maintains structured philanthropic programs through its Wyndham Worldwide Foundation, supporting signature charities in children's health, education, and wellness. Focus areas include conservation, education, wellness, and human trafficking prevention with employee donation matching and voluntary point donations through loyalty programs.
charity.amt: Not publicly disclosed; estimated millions annually through foundation and employee programs
charity.focus: Children's health, education, conservation, veteran support, human rights/anti-trafficking

environment.s: Wyndham Green Program provides five-level certification addressing energy, water, waste, and biodiversity. Company commits to 15% absolute carbon reduction by 2025 and 100% renewable energy at corporate HQ and North American offices. Participates in CDP climate responses and achieved management-band sustainability rating.

labor.s: Wyndham emphasizes inclusive workplace culture with DEI training and gender pay equity (98% at executive levels). However, franchising model limits direct company control over hotel labor practices. No major OSHA/NLRB violations reported, though as franchisor, actual labor conditions vary by franchisee.

dei.s: Wyndham achieved perfect scores on HRC Corporate Equality Index for four consecutive years and recognition by DiversityInc and Forbes for leading diversity practices. Programs include Women Own the Room for female entrepreneurs, BOLD for Black entrepreneurs, and comprehensive DEI training across organization.

animals.s: Not applicable to hotel franchising business.

guns.s: Not applicable to hotel franchising business.

privacy.s: Wyndham faces significant privacy concerns including historical FTC data breaches (2008-2010) involving 619,000+ payment card accounts and 2026 investigation into Meta pixel tracking and data sharing with Facebook without clear consent. Company settled FTC enforcement requiring 20-year security program oversight.

execPay.s: CEO Geoffrey Ballotti earned $13.9M in 2024 (90.7% performance-based), significantly above peer average of $8.15M. CEO compensation increased 20% while company earnings declined 20% in past year, indicating misalignment between executive pay and performance.
